content.summary: Uses the L-system SOP to generate procedural branching and growth patterns based on Lindenmayer system rules. By defining production rules, angle parameters, and generation count, you can create organic tree structures, coral-like forms, and fractal branching geometry. The output can be rendered directly, used as a path for particle systems, or converted to tubes for 3D organic shapes.

codeSnippets.label: Configure L-system SOP for a branching tree
codeSnippets.code:
# Set up an L-system SOP with tree-like branching rules
lsys = op('lsystem1')

# Basic tree rules
# F = draw forward, + = turn right, - = turn left
# [ = push state (start branch), ] = pop state (end branch)
lsys.par.premise = 'F'
lsys.par.rule1 = 'F=FF-[-F+F+F]+[+F-F-F]'
lsys.par.generations = 4
lsys.par.angle = 22.5
lsys.par.stepl = 0.3

# Animate growth by changing generations over time
# lsys.par.generations.expr = 'int(absTime.seconds % 6) + 1'
codeSnippets.description: Configures a classic L-system tree with branching rules, 22.5-degree branching angle, and 4 generations of growth.

codeSnippets.label: Convert L-system lines to renderable tubes
codeSnippets.code:
# Convert L-system wireframe to 3D tubes
tube = op('tube_from_lsys')
tube.par.primtype = 'mesh'
tube.par.cols = 6  # 6-sided tubes
tube.par.rad1 = 0.02
tube.par.rad2 = 0.01  # Taper toward tips

# Add facet SOP for proper normals
facet = op('facet1')
facet.par.unique = True
facet.par.consolidate = True
codeSnippets.description: Converts L-system line segments into tapered 3D tubes suitable for rendering.

tips: Start with low generation counts (2-3) and increase gradually — L-systems grow exponentially in complexity.
Use the angle parameter to dramatically change the character of the branching: 22.5 for trees, 60 for hexagonal, 90 for square grids.
Animate the step length with time to create growing/shrinking effects.
Add a Noise SOP after the L-system to add organic randomness to the otherwise perfect fractal structure.
Use different rules for 2D (+ and -) versus 3D (add & ^ /) branching patterns.
